content: Just as human language learners build long-term conceptual understanding through repeated explicit reflection on writing output, large language models can combine the structured iterative self-feedback of Self-Refine with the dynamic long-term memory architecture of Reflexion to avoid the common pitfall of discarding hard-won incremental improvements between refinement iterations. This creates a hybrid agent framework that retains both the focused step-by-step output improvement of Self-Refine and the cumulative long-term learning of general task competence from Reflexion.
